ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers poultry gizzard; adapted to duck gizzard braised preparation as no exact duck-specific braised code exists in the public FoodEx2 browser. 'closest_parent' used as it's a general poultry gizzard code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept for duck gizzard identified in the public SNOMED CT browser.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of braised duck gizzards is approximately 100 g, equivalent to 4–6 pieces depending on size, commonly served as a side dish or appetiser in a Singaporean Chinese meal.

nutrient_source.title: Duck Gizzards 鸭胗 — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Duck Gizzards 鸭胗 (cr_id G00010779). All per-100g values are as reported; confidence 0.95 per source metadata.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Sweet Braised Dishes — Analogous estimate based on sweetened soy-braised preparations; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for braised duck gizzards. The dish contains 60.7 g/100g carbohydrate of which 30.5 g is sugar, derived from a heavily sweetened soy-braising sauce. GI estimated at 65 (medium) by analogy with sweetened soy-braised preparations and sugar-dominant sauces documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (65 × 60.66) / 100 = 39.4 g — classified as very high GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 712 mg/100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g high-concern threshold; compounded by very high sugar content (30.5 g/100 g) which independently elevates blood pressure via insulin-mediated sodium retention.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this preparation; if duck gizzards are desired, request a low-sodium, unsweetened preparation and limit portion to no more than 50 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 60.7 g/100 g with sugar 30.5 g/100 g and dietary fibre only 0.6 g/100 g; estimated GI 65 yields a glycemic load of approximately 39 per 100 g serving — extremely high for blood glucose management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this sweetened braised preparation entirely; if gizzards are consumed, they must be prepared without added sugar or sweet sauces and portion size must be strictly limited.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (712 mg/100 g) raises blood pressure and vascular load; very high sugar (30.5 g/100 g) promotes hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial inflammation; low fat content (0.13 g/100 g) does not mitigate the cardiometabolic risk from sodium and sugar.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this preparation due to its high sodium and sugar content, both of which are independent risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Duck Gizzards (鸭胗, Ya Zhen) is a braised duck offal dish popular in Singaporean Chinese cuisine, commonly served as a side dish, appetiser, or hawker centre accompaniment. The gizzard — the muscular stomach of the duck — is cleaned, marinated, and slow-braised in a heavily sweetened soy-based sauce containing soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, and aromatic spices such as star anise and cinnamon. The result is a dense, chewy, dark-coloured piece of meat with a rich, sweet-savoury flavour profile. Per 100 g, this preparation provides 245 kcal, 60.7 g carbohydrate (of which 30.5 g is sugar), only 0.36 g protein, and 0.13 g fat — an unusual macronutrient profile reflecting the dominance of the braising sauce rather than the gizzard tissue itself. Sodium is 712 mg/100 g, driven by soy sauce and oyster sauce. The dish is not suitable for individuals managing blood glucose, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its extremely high sugar and sodium content. Dietary fibre is minimal at 0.6 g/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 65 (medium) with a glycemic load of approximately 39 per 100 g — classified as very high. Cholesterol is reported as 0 mg/100 g per the HPB source, which is atypical for offal and likely reflects the sauce-dominant composition of the analysed sample. This dish is not halal-certified by default as duck gizzards require specific halal slaughter certification.
common_user_queries: How many calories in braised duck gizzards?
Is duck gizzard high in sodium?
Is duck gizzard suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010779) with confidence 0.95 per source metadata. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate (confidence medium) as no direct GI measurement exists for this preparation.
notes: 1. protein_g is 0.36 g/100 g — this is an unusually low value for a meat-based dish and reflects the sauce-dominant composition of the HPB-analysed sample; the value is as reported by HPB and is not zero. fat_g is 0.13 g/100 g — similarly low, reflecting the lean nature of gizzard tissue and the sauce-dominant sample; the value is as reported by HPB and is not zero. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g by HPB — this is atypical for offal; confirmed zero per HPB source. 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this braised duck gizzard preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for G00010779 and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent braised preparation as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 65, medium) based on sweetened soy-braised preparations from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed journal; no direct GI measurement for braised duck gizzards exists in any approved GI source. 4. FoodEx2 code A0FKP is adapted from the poultry gizzard category; no exact duck-specific braised gizzard code exists in the public FoodEx2 browser. 5. Halal status is unconfirmed — duck gizzards require specific halal slaughter certification; is_halal set to false pending certification evidence. 6. The nightshade caution in the original input health_suitability for rheumatoid_arthritis has been removed as there is no evidence of nightshade ingredients in this dish.
